name: framer-motion
summary: Production-ready animations and gestures for React — declarative, physics-based, with layout animations and exit transitions.

story.problem: CSS animations are hard to coordinate with React state. You want animations that respond to data changes, animate elements in/out, drag gestures, shared element transitions, and scroll-linked effects — all without managing timelines manually.
story.mental_model: Replace any HTML element with its motion equivalent: <motion.div>. Add animate={{ opacity: 1, x: 0 }} and initial={{ opacity: 0, x: -20 }} and framer-motion handles the tween. AnimatePresence wraps conditionally rendered children to give them exit animations. layoutId enables shared element transitions.
story.when_to_use: React apps needing UI animations: page transitions, list reordering, drag-and-drop, hover effects, scroll-triggered reveals, and exit animations.
story.when_not_to_use: Non-React projects, performance-critical animations running at 120fps with many particles (use CSS or WebGL), or very simple opacity/transform transitions where CSS is sufficient.

alternatives.name: motion
alternatives.reason: The newer, framework-agnostic version of framer-motion (same team)

alternatives.name: react-spring
alternatives.reason: Physics-based, no keyframes — different mental model

alternatives.name: gsap
alternatives.reason: Framework-agnostic, more control, better for complex timelines

nodes.label: AnimatePresence
nodes.summary: Enable exit animations for conditionally rendered children

nodes.label: useAnimation()
nodes.summary: Programmatically control animations via a controls object

nodes.label: useScroll()
nodes.summary: Track scroll position as a MotionValue

nodes.label: layoutId
nodes.summary: Shared element transition between route/state changes

nodes.label: drag prop
nodes.summary: Make any element draggable with constraints

nodes.label: Variants
nodes.summary: Named animation states that cascade to children
